task_completion.description: Verify that the original color image is opened in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible with a color image (either a landscape or a butterfly) loaded in the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent accesses the Desaturate tool or changes the image mode to Grayscale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A GIMP menu or dialog related to color removal is visible, such as the 'Desaturate' dialog box (Colors > Desaturate) or the Image > Mode menu showing 'Grayscale'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the image on the canvas has been successfully converted to grayscale.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in the GIMP canvas no longer contains any color; it appears entirely in black, white, and gray tones.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opens the Export dialog to save the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog box is visible on the screen (typically accessed via File > Export As).

task_completion.description: Verify that the image is exported to the exact specified path: /home/ga/Documents/output.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Export dialog, the Name field shows 'output.png' and the destination folder path clearly indicates '/home/ga/Documents'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the conversion rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with GIMP menus, dialogs, and the canvas. There should be no terminal window executing image processing commands like 'convert' or 'mogrify'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually modified the image in GIMP rather than downloading or opening a pre-made grayscale image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show a clear transition of the same image from color to grayscale within the GIMP canvas.

integrity.description: Ensure the file was saved via GIMP's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP 'Export Image' dialog must be used to save the file, rather than using terminal commands like 'mv' or 'cp' to rename an existing file to output.png.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image is a real, high-resolution Unsplash photo (either a landscape at 1000x667 or a butterfly at 800x1200). The agent is expected to use GIMP 2.10 to perform the task. A successful conversion means the image completely loses all color (saturation becomes 0, and RGB channels become identical). The final output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/output.png'.
